items.headline: City Council reviews youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: The council received status updates on San José's Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including youth empowerment programs and summer safety initiatives, plus a Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These reports outline how the city is investing in youth services and modernizing library infrastructure.

items.headline: Downtown building gets historic upgrade and 5,000 sq ft bonus
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This could enable significant expansion or redevelopment of the downtown property.

items.headline: City launches summer jobs program for young residents
items.summary: Cupertino presented a Summer Jobs program to provide employment opportunities for local youth. The initiative aims to offer work experience and income for Cupertino residents during the summer months.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A rezoning proposal would convert service commercial land at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story mixed-use building with 167 residential units (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The city is reviewing the conceptual plan on April 8th.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: A scheduled administrative zoning hearing for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled, pushing back a project at 333 Franklin Street to a later date to be announced. Residents interested in this development will need to watch for new hearing notices.

items.headline: Figure AI expands San José presence with major office buildout
items.summary: The AI robotics company Figure AI is investing $4.1M in a second-floor office buildout at 4000 N 1st St, signaling continued growth in San José's tech sector. This follows major tech tenant improvements elsewhere in the city.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda does not specify the project details. Residents should monitor follow-up reports to learn what infrastructure or improvements this contract will fund.

items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza arts and culture school
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding will help expand cultural and educational programming at the plaza.
